evals.name: full-landscape-survey
evals.prompt: What are all the design patterns used to build agentic AI systems? Give me the full picture.
evals.expected_output: Presents the complete map: all eight families (reasoning, planning, workflow orchestration, multi-agent, tool/action, RAG, memory, control/safety) with every pattern named and a one-line essence each. Comprehensive coverage over depth. Offers to drill into any family/pattern/framework.

evals.name: compare-two-patterns
evals.prompt: Can you explain the difference between ReAct, Plan-and-Execute, and ReWOO? When would I use each?
evals.expected_output: Explains each conceptually (ReAct = interleaved think/act per step; Plan-and-Execute = plan all steps then execute with re-planning; ReWOO = plan all tool calls up front then reason once). Compares on adaptivity vs. token cost, and gives when-to-use guidance. Situates them in the planning/reasoning families and notes where they live in the ecosystem (e.g. LangGraph tutorials).

evals.name: framework-pattern-coverage
evals.prompt: Which agentic design patterns does LangGraph support, and how does that compare to CrewAI and the OpenAI Agents SDK?
evals.expected_output: Maps each framework to the patterns it supports and its vocabulary: LangGraph (graph/explicit, nearly all control-flow patterns, best HITL/persistence), CrewAI (role/declarative teams, supervisor/hierarchical), OpenAI Agents SDK (handoff/swarm multi-agent, guardrails). Frames the graph-vs-role spectrum. Stays conceptual (understanding coverage), not a build tutorial.
